name: Mora de Rubielos - Rubielos de Mora – circular by the Mijares river
name_original: Mora de Rubielos - Rubielos de Mora – circular por el río Mijares
region_intro: Touring cycling around Albentosa offers routes through varied landscapes in the province of Teruel, Spain. The region is characterized by its position along the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, a greenway featuring historical railway infrastructure. Cyclists encounter riverbanks, viaducts, and tunnels, with terrain that includes both gradual ascents and flatter sections. The area provides a mix of engaging and challenging routes for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Albentosa The most popular touring cycling route is Mora de Rubielos - Rubielos de Mora – circular by the Mijares river, a 32.6 miles (52.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 55 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows the Mijares river, offering scenic views. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vía Verde de Ojos Negros – circular from Mora de Rubielos, a moderate 19.2 miles (30.9 km) path. This route utilizes a section of the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, known for its well-maintained surface and historical features. Local touring cyclists also love the Red earth – View of Barracas loop from Mora de Rubielos, a 38.1 miles (61.3 km) trail leading through diverse terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 50 minutes. Touring cycling around Albentosa is defined by the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, historical viaducts, and riverbanks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate greenway sections to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Albentosa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 50 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Albentosa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Albentosa?
A: There are over 50 touring cycling routes around Albentosa, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. You'll find everything from easy greenway sections to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain.

Q: What is the general terrain like for touring cycling in Albentosa?
A: The terrain around Albentosa is diverse, characterized by its position along the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros. Cyclists will encounter riverbanks, historical viaducts, and tunnels. The Vía Verde itself offers well-maintained surfaces, while other routes can include both gradual ascents and flatter sections, providing varied experiences.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several easier routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31506806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Red earth – Palancar Station Campsite loop from Rubielos de Mora&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option. The Vía Verde de Ojos Negros also features sections that are generally well-maintained and suitable for less experienced cyclists or families.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or scenic viewpoints along the cycling routes?
A: Cyclists can enjoy several prominent features. The Viaduct of Albentosa offers panoramic views of the Albentosa River banks and the town. The Vía Verde de Ojos Negros itself includes the Albentosa Tunnel, the longest on the route. You might also encounter natural features like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1756156" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Red earth&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4374269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4374121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carlos IV Bridge&lt;/a&gt; is another historical point of interest.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955806404/via-verde-de-ojos-negros-circular-from-mora-de-rubielos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de Ojos Negros – circular from Mora de Rubielos&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular path. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956714546/mora-de-rubielos-rubielos-de-mora-circular-by-the-mijares-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mora de Rubielos - Rubielos de Mora – circular by the Mijares river&lt;/a&gt;, which is a more difficult loop.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Albentosa?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied landscapes, the well-maintained sections of the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, and the mix of engaging and challenging routes available.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Albentosa?
A: While specific seasonal recommendations aren't provided, the region's diverse terrain suggests that spring and autumn would likely offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling. Summers can be hot in Teruel province, and winters might bring colder conditions, especially at higher altitudes along the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, which ascends to 1,240 meters at Puerto Escandón.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly cycling routes in Albentosa?
A: Many natural trails and greenways, including sections of the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, are generally dog-friendly, provided dogs are kept under control, typically on a leash. It's always advisable to check local regulations for specific routes and ensure your dog is comfortable with the distance and terrain.

Q: Where can I find places to eat or rest along the cycling routes?
A: Albentosa itself offers cafes and restaurants. Along some routes, you might find facilities like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813500" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palancar Station Campsite&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2614427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albentosa Picnic Area&lt;/a&gt;. For longer rides, it's recommended to plan your stops in towns like Mora de Rubielos or Rubielos de Mora, which are starting points for several tours.

Q: Is public transport available to reach Albentosa with a bike?
A: Albentosa is a key point along the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros. While specific public transport options for cyclists aren't detailed, train services often run along former railway lines that greenways utilize. It's best to check regional bus and train schedules for services to Albentosa or nearby towns like Mora de Rubielos, and confirm their bike carriage policies.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Albentosa offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40336948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa de la Estación loop from Mora de Rubielos&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging option with significant elevation gain. Another difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33727418" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Red earth – View of Barracas loop from Mora de Rubielos&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 60 km through diverse terrain.
tips: We are in the small town of Olba, in the Gúdar-Javalambre region of the province of Teruel and it is certainly worth a visit. This is a stunning location with dramatic panoramic views over the Area. The Sierra Javalambre are clearly seen with the ski station and observatory visible on a clear day.

This is a beautiful medieval walled village sitting between the mountains ranges of the Sierra Espedan and the Gudar. It is a popular weekend break location for Valencian families and somewhere to stay close to both local ski stations (Valdelinares and Javalambre)

Both to eat and to stay! It's a great place, very nice people who give you great tips and 'tricks' about the Via Verde and the area in general. At a gastronomic level a 10, go fabric as they work the truffle and at a more than correct price.
